text:
(a) A person who does not bring suit within the period prescribed by Section 315.092 (Suit to Contest Assessment or Proceeding) or who does not diligently and in good faith prosecute a suit to final judgment may not contest or raise as a defense in another action the validity of a proceeding or an assessment, lien, or personal liability imposed by the proceeding.

(b) Estoppel under this section binds a person’s heirs, successors, administrators, and assigns.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 315. Artificial Lighting
##### § 315.093 — Estoppel

(a) A person who does not bring suit within the period prescribed by Section 315.092 (Suit to Contest Assessment or Proceeding) or who does not diligently and in good faith prosecute a suit to final judgment may not contest or raise as a defense in another action the validity of a proceeding or an assessment, lien, or personal liability imposed by the proceeding.

(b) Estoppel under this section binds a person’s heirs, successors, administrators, and assigns.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Estoppel under this section binds a person’s heirs, successors, administrators, and assigns.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
